name: GreenEarth Cleaning

overview.summary: GreenEarth Cleaning is a licensing-based company that promotes and supports the use of liquid silicone (D5 — decamethylcyclopentasiloxane) as a dry cleaning solvent. Founded in 1999 by General Electric, Procter & Gamble, and three independent dry cleaners, GreenEarth operates as a licensing network rather than a traditional chemical manufacturer. With 1,800+ affiliated cleaning plants in 40+ countries and 6,000+ retail service points, they are the dominant force in 'green' dry cleaning marketing. Recently acquired by Greybull Stewardship, L.P.
overview.historyAndReputation: GreenEarth launched in 1999 with massive corporate backing from GE and P&G — both had vested interests in silicone chemistry (GE manufactured silicones, P&G used them in consumer products). The founding trio of independent dry cleaners (Jim Barry, Ron Benjamin, Jim Douglas) provided industry credibility. GreenEarth invested $30 million in independent testing and safety research. The brand has become synonymous with 'environmentally friendly dry cleaning' among consumers, though the D5 solvent's environmental profile is debated in scientific literature.
overview.marketPosition: Market leader in the 'green' dry cleaning segment. GreenEarth is not a traditional chemical company — they don't manufacture products. They license a brand, a solvent system, and a marketing platform. Their strength is in consumer-facing messaging and brand recognition. Affiliated cleaners benefit from the GreenEarth name's environmental positioning. Pricing is at a premium due to licensing fees and specialized equipment requirements.

specialties: Licensing and branding network for 'green' cleaning

productLines.categoryDescription: GreenEarth's entire business centers on one solvent: D5 liquid silicone. Unlike traditional chemical companies that offer multiple solvents, GreenEarth is a single-solvent system.

products.name: GreenEarth Silicone Solvent (D5)
products.description: 100% pure liquid silicone derived from silica (sand). Clear, odorless, non-toxic, chemically inert. D5 does NOT interact with textile fibers or dyes during cleaning — it carries away soil without chemically attacking the garment. This preserves fabric softness, prevents shrinkage, and eliminates color loss. The same silicone chemistry used in consumer cosmetics and personal care products.
usedFor: primary dry cleaning solvent in GreenEarth-licensed machines
fibers: all fibers — extremely gentle due to chemical inertness
notFor: heavily soiled items may need additional pre-treatment due to D5's gentler cleaning action
products.proTips: D5's chemical inertness is both its strength and limitation. It won't damage anything — but it also won't dissolve stubborn stains as aggressively as perc or SENSENE™. Pre-spotting becomes more important in a GreenEarth system. Pair with Sustain™ detergent and GreenSPOT® (from R.R. Street) for best results.
products.alternativeTo: Perchloroethylene, hydrocarbon (DF-2000), Kreussler SolvonK4, Seitz SENSENE™

productLines.categoryDescription: Detergents formulated specifically for the D5 silicone solvent system. D5 requires different detergent chemistry than perc or hydrocarbon — standard detergents don't dissolve properly in silicone.

products.name: Sustain™
products.description: Proprietary detergent specifically formulated for D5 silicone solvent. Biodegradable, phosphate-free. Enhances cleaning performance beyond what D5 alone achieves. The primary detergent recommended by GreenEarth for their system.
notFor: perc or hydrocarbon systems — incompatible chemistry
products.proTips: D5 is a weak solvent compared to perc — it relies more on the detergent for actual cleaning. Under-dosing Sustain™ is the most common cause of poor cleaning results in GreenEarth systems.
products.alternativeTo: R.R. Street GreenSOL®, Kreussler CLIP for GreenEarth

products.name: SANITONE® 8870 Detergent
products.description: Surfactant-optimized detergent formulation for D5 systems. Leaves garments with smooth, soft hand. Enhances soil penetration and flushing. An alternative detergent option within the GreenEarth ecosystem.
products.proTips: SANITONE 8870 emphasizes hand feel — garments come out noticeably softer than with some other D5 detergents. Good for operators whose customers prioritize fabric feel.

agentMapping.NSD: Sustain™ and SANITONE® 8870 (detergents, not standalone spotters). GreenEarth relies on third-party spotting agents — typically R.R. Street GreenSPOT® and GreenSOL®.
agentMapping.POG: No GreenEarth-branded POG. Use R.R. Street PICRIN® or A.L. Wilson TarGo® for pre-spotting in GreenEarth systems.
agentMapping.protein: No GreenEarth-branded protein spotter. Use R.R. Street StreePRO® or A.L. Wilson QwikGo®.
agentMapping.tannin: No GreenEarth-branded tannin spotter. Use R.R. Street StreeTAN® or A.L. Wilson BonGo®.
agentMapping.leveling: No GreenEarth-branded leveling agent. Use R.R. Street GreenSPOT® (formulated for GreenEarth systems).

fiberExpertise.silk: D5 is exceptionally gentle on silk due to chemical inertness — no interaction with silk protein fibers. No shrinkage, no color loss, no dye bleeding. One of the safest solvents for silk garments. The trade-off is less aggressive stain removal — pre-spotting is essential.
fiberExpertise.wool: Same advantages as silk — D5 doesn't interact with wool keratin. No felting risk from the solvent itself (felting requires heat + agitation + alkalinity, none of which D5 contributes). Safe for wool.
fiberExpertise.leather: D5 is gentle on leather. GreenEarth markets leather cleaning as a capability, though dedicated leather products (Royaltone, Kreussler LANADOL LICKER) may provide better conditioning results.
fiberExpertise.synthetics: D5 handles synthetics well. The chemical inertness means no fiber damage. Less effective at dissolving oil-based soils trapped in synthetic fibers compared to higher-KB solvents like SENSENE™.
fiberExpertise.delicates: D5's primary selling point is gentleness on delicates. The solvent doesn't interact with fibers or dyes at all — it just carries soil away mechanically. This makes it theoretically the safest solvent for any delicate garment. The practical limitation is stain removal power.

protocols.name: GreenEarth Standard Cleaning
protocols.description: Pre-spot with GreenSPOT® (R.R. Street) on collars, cuffs, and stained areas. Load garments into GreenEarth-licensed machine with Sustain™ or SANITONE® 8870 detergent. Standard cleaning cycle. D5's gentleness means fewer damage claims but more pre-spotting labor.
protocols.usedFor: Standard professional dry cleaning in GreenEarth systems

distributionNetwork.distributionMethod: licensing/affiliate model — operators pay annual license fees to use GreenEarth brand and system
distributionNetwork.howToBuy: Apply to become a GreenEarth affiliate through greenearthcleaning.com. GreenEarth does NOT sell solvent or equipment directly — they license the system and connect affiliates with equipment manufacturers and chemical suppliers. Annual licensing fee required.

strengthsKnownFor: Dominant brand in 'green' dry cleaning marketing
Non-toxic, non-carcinogenic solvent (D5 is far safer than perc for workers)
Corporate founding partners (GE, P&G) provided initial research credibility
$30 million invested in independent safety and environmental testing
1,800+ affiliated plants, 6,000+ retail service points — massive network
Strong consumer brand recognition for environmental positioning
D5 is exceptionally gentle on all fiber types
Lower energy costs (50%+ reduction claimed vs. traditional systems)
Fewer garment damage claims due to solvent inertness
limitations: D5 (decamethylcyclopentasiloxane) classified by EU as 'very persistent' and 'very bioaccumulative'
Massachusetts TURI ranks D5 poorly among perc alternatives due to environmental persistence
D5 accumulates in aquatic ecosystems, particularly in fish — long-term impact uncertain
Weaker cleaning power than perc, hydrocarbon, or SENSENE™ — relies heavily on pre-spotting and detergent
No GreenEarth-branded spotting agents — operators must source from other suppliers
Licensing model adds cost on top of solvent and equipment investment
GreenEarth is a marketing/licensing company, not a chemical manufacturer — limited technical depth
EU regulatory scrutiny on D4/D5/D6 siloxanes continues
10-year EU derogation for GreenEarth use may not be renewed
industryReputation.whoUsesThem: Dry cleaners marketing to environmentally conscious consumers. Boutique and high-end cleaners in health-conscious markets (California, Northeast). Operations in buildings with strict VOC regulations where perc is prohibited. Cleaners who prioritize zero garment damage claims over maximum stain removal. Franchise-style operations that benefit from the GreenEarth brand umbrella.

education.trainingResources: Affiliate portal with video training, documents, product ordering, and promotional materials. GreenEarth provides marketing support and consumer education materials to affiliates.
education.technicalSupport: Support through affiliate program. Equipment manufacturers provide machine-specific technical support. Chemical suppliers (R.R. Street, etc.) provide spotting and detergent support.
education.publications: Consumer-facing environmental education materials. Affiliate marketing toolkit. FAQ resources on greenearthcleaning.com.

gonrIntegration.relevance: GreenEarth is relevant when a GONR user operates in or interacts with a GreenEarth-affiliated dry cleaner. Understanding that D5 is a gentle but less powerful solvent helps set expectations: more pre-spotting needed, more reliance on detergent, fewer aggressive stain removal options. GONR's safety-first approach aligns well with GreenEarth's gentleness, but GONR protocols should note when stains may require stronger solvents than D5.
gonrIntegration.agentCrossReference: GreenEarth does not have branded spotting agents. For GreenEarth systems, use: R.R. Street GreenSPOT® (pre-treatment), GreenSOL® (detergent alternative), StreeTAN® (tannin), StreePRO® (protein), PICRIN® (POG); or A.L. Wilson equivalents. Detergent: Sustain™ or SANITONE® 8870.
gonrIntegration.whenToRecommend: Recommend GreenEarth when: the protocol calls for the gentlest possible solvent cleaning (irreplaceable garments, extreme delicates); the operator is in a market that prohibits perc; the customer specifically requests 'green' or 'eco-friendly' dry cleaning; or the garment has minimal staining and primarily needs refreshing rather than aggressive stain removal. Note limitations when heavy staining requires stronger solvents.
